title: Supreme Court Weighs Trump's Birthright Citizenship Directive

scenes.subtitle_text: According to the Associated Press, the Supreme Court appears skeptical of Trump's directive to restrict birthright citizenship, a key part of his immigration policy
scenes.image_search_query: US Supreme Court building real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: Trump attended the Supreme Court hearing, marking the first time a sitting president has been present for oral arguments
scenes.image_search_query: Trump arriving at the Supreme Court press photo

scenes.subtitle_text: Solicitor General D John Sauer argued that birthright citizenship demeans American citizenship and encourages illegal immigration
scenes.image_search_query: lawyer arguing case courtroom real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: Chief Justice Roberts questioned the administration's argument, calling it 'quirky' and challenging the evidence for 'birth tourism'
scenes.image_search_query: Chief Justice John Roberts press photo

scenes.subtitle_text: Justice Kagan argued that the administration's interpretation of the 14th Amendment is not supported by the text
scenes.image_search_query: Justice Elena Kagan giving speech real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: The ACLU attorney argued that the 14th Amendment enshrined birthright citizenship, protecting it from governmental overreach
The Supreme Court's decision, expected by June, could impact hundreds of thousands of births annually, potentially requiring families to prove citizenship
